lenses.guidance: 'By my name Yahweh I did not make myself known' (v 3) — Hebrew yada' ('know') is relational, not informational. The original audience hears: the patriarchs experienced God; this generation will hear his proper name in action.

The sevenfold 'I will' (vv 6-8) compresses the redemptive promise: bring out, deliver, redeem, take, be your God, bring in, give. The covenant arc fits in one paragraph; every later act of salvation traces back to these verses.

v 3's 'by my name Yahweh I did not make myself known' has been debated across Scripture — does it contradict Gen 17:1's El Shaddai? The sevenfold 'I will' is the canonical answer: the name is revealed by the action it performs.

'I will redeem you with an outstretched arm' (v 6) is taken up at Lk 1:51 (Mary's Magnificat) and applied to Christ's saving work. The deliverer of Israel becomes the deliverer of all who trust in him.

Israel cannot hear because of 'broken spirit and harsh bondage' (v 9) — pain blocks faith's reception. Pray with this in mind for those whose suffering has made them deaf to comfort. Trust the slow work of restoration.
